# Transcrição: 2.9_Watch_Before_Applying_With_Our_Recruiting_Team

- **Fonte:** 2.9_Watch_Before_Applying_With_Our_Recruiting_Team
- **Duração:** 02:52
- **Idioma:** en

---

## [00:00 - 02:52]

So guys, cool here. Quick few notes before you apply in our recruiting pool. First of all, you have to complete the entire training with full comprehension of the sales process, particularly intro and getting hired week, the ramp up, sales process part one, sales process part two, and the prospecting week. So the weeks you can kind of lay off of a little bit. You don't need to know necessarily the philosophy like the back of your hand or the intergame like the back of your hand. Those are just good trainings to go through time and time again. The objections, you don't need to know like the back of your hand, even though you should have gone through it. And then the follow up as well is one where you don't need to know that that well right now. Now, don't get me wrong, all of this stuff will be a game changer for you as you advance in your career. But these weeks right here are like the fundamental ones I want you to really have down. And you will be quizzed on these. Now, you'll also need to attend a minimum of six group coaching calls a week and get a minimum of one call reviewed on those group coaching calls. Now, we do track all of that stuff so we know. And this doesn't mean you should stop attending the calls at six or that you should only have one call review. In both cases, that's just the minimum. You should get as many as you can. And the more active you are with attending the calls and call reviews, the better results you're going to get and the more competitive you're going to be in the pool. Also, conduct 30 sales calls and or mark triage call role plays with three to five plus partners on or off the group calls minimum. So I know sometimes you guys break into groups and you do role plays in different groups. So that counts here. But some of these should be off the call partnering up with somebody else in the program as well. And we want to go for at least 30 of these with three to five different partners. Now, I would do a whole lot more than this, but the minimum for you guys is at least 30. Now, when you're doing these, both focus on the entire call and I would drill specific parts that you're having trouble with. Then I would personally break down a minimum of 10 of your own role play calls and then have three recordings of your best role play calls to submit to our team. So these are the requirements our recruiting team is going to ask you for. Now, this doesn't mean, by the way, that you should only depend on our recruiting. As you saw earlier in this training in the landing interviews, you can go out there and farm and land interviews like crazy if you just take a lot of action. So you should head your bets with that. Stack the deck in your favor. Get as many interviews you can on your own. And obviously, apply with our recruiting team as well. We want to get you placed if we can get you placed. Obviously, you have to be able to perform. You have to go through the training. You have to take actions and do all of your part as well. But if you do, you shouldn't have any trouble, especially if you do all of these guys, you should be fine. So that's it for this training, guys, and we'll see you in the next training.
